title: Mangrove Carbon
script_summary: An insightful look into the science of mangrove carbon and its uncertain impact on climate change.

segments.voiceover: What if I told you mangroves hold secrets to our climate fate?
Mangroves are unique coastal forests that capture carbon dioxide from the atmosphere.
But how much carbon do they really store? The science isn't as clear as you think.
Studies show mangroves can sequester up to five times more carbon than tropical forests.
Yet, climate variability makes their long-term impact uncertain.
So, should we plant more? Or are we risking too much?
Dive deeper into mangrove science! Follow for more insights.
